How Babur used village homes as a shield in the Battle of PanipatIt has been 500 years since the first Battle of Panipat was fought, and it is remembered mainly for the first use of cannons in India, and the beginning of the Mughal empire. However, some literature suggests that the conquest was built not only on gunpowder and cavalry, but on civilians caught between his and Ibrahim Lodi's armies.

Live: Iran's Araghchi holds calls with Asim Munir on ceasefire, says state mediaUS-Iran War Live Updates: The situation in the Middle East remains tense as military activity and diplomatic efforts continue at the same time. The USS George HW Bush has arrived in the region, increasing the number of US aircraft carriers in operation there to three. Donald Trump has stated that any deal with Iran will be made only when it is "appropriate and good" for the United States. Iranian leaders, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, have rejected claims that there are divisions within the country's leadership. The US has taken further military steps. The US President has ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill" Iranian boats suspected of placing mines in the Strait of Hormuz. The US military has also intensified mine-clearing operations in the waterway. In addition, the Pentagon said US forces boarded the tanker Majestic X in the Indian Ocean. These developments come after the US extended a ceasefire with Iran, although there is no formal extension or confirmed timeline for further talks. Diplomatic efforts are continuing alongside military developments. The United States is hosting a second round of talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington. The discussions are focused on extending a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Both sides have carried out strikes during the truce period. So far, there is no clear deadline for ending the conflict or for reaching a new agreement. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

Most IITians went abroad: Zoho CEO Sridhar Vembu's 80s brain drain post hits a nerveZoho founder Sridhar Vembu's viral post on leaving India in 1989 has reopened the IIT brain drain debate. He said most IITians went abroad because opportunities were limited and the country felt stuck. His post is now resonating widely with Indians discussing talent, growth and nation-building.

Pakistan's peace virus leads to lockdowns in twin cities, fuels public angerPakistan's bid to broker US-Iran peace talks is increasingly backfiring at home.
